feed.title: Pronounce — recent dictionary additions
feed.subtitle: How engineers actually pronounce project / product / jargon names. Community-maintained.

entry.title: PoV — P O V
entry.summary: PoV is pronounced "P O V" (/ˌpiː oʊ ˈviː/). Letters "P-O-V". Proof of Value (a deeper, contract-stage PoC).

entry.title: ABM — A B M
entry.summary: ABM is pronounced "A B M" (/ˌeɪ biː ˈɛm/). Letters "A-B-M". Account-Based Marketing.

entry.title: moat — moht
entry.summary: moat is pronounced "moht" (/moʊt/). "moht" — rhymes with boat. Sustainable competitive advantage (Warren Buffett).

entry.title: flywheel — FLY wheel
entry.summary: flywheel is pronounced "FLY wheel" (/ˈflaɪ wiːl/). "FLY-wheel". Self-reinforcing growth loop (Jim Collins / Bezos).

entry.title: COGS — kogs
entry.summary: COGS is pronounced "kogs" (/kɒɡz/). "kogs" — rhymes with logs. Cost of Goods Sold.

entry.title: CRM — C R M
entry.summary: CRM is pronounced "C R M" (/ˌsiː ɑːr ˈɛm/). Letters "C-R-M". Customer Relationship Management.

entry.title: ERP — E R P
entry.summary: ERP is pronounced "E R P" (/ˌiː ɑːr ˈpiː/). "E-R-P" letter-by-letter standard; "urp" (rhymes with burp) heard in some industries. Enterprise Resource Planning.

entry.title: EBITDA — EE bit dah
entry.summary: EBITDA is pronounced "EE bit dah" (/ˈiː bɪt dɑː/). "EE-bit-dah". Earnings Before Interest, Taxes, Depreciation, Amortization.

entry.title: SOC2 — sock two
entry.summary: SOC2 is pronounced "sock two" (/ˌsɒk ˈtuː/). "sock-two". AICPA service-organization audit standard.

entry.title: SOX — sox
entry.summary: SOX is pronounced "sox" (/sɒks/). "sox" — rhymes with box. Sarbanes-Oxley Act (US public-company compliance).

entry.title: SEO — S E O
entry.summary: SEO is pronounced "S E O" (/ˌɛs iː ˈoʊ/). Letters "S-E-O". Search Engine Optimization.

entry.title: SEM — S E M
entry.summary: SEM is pronounced "S E M" (/ˌɛs iː ˈɛm/). Letters "S-E-M". Search Engine Marketing (paid ads).

entry.title: CTR — C T R
entry.summary: CTR is pronounced "C T R" (/ˌsiː tiː ˈɑːr/). Letters "C-T-R". Click-Through Rate.

entry.title: CPC — C P C
entry.summary: CPC is pronounced "C P C" (/ˌsiː piː ˈsiː/). Letters "C-P-C". Cost Per Click.

entry.title: CPM — C P M
entry.summary: CPM is pronounced "C P M" (/ˌsiː piː ˈɛm/). Letters "C-P-M". Cost Per Mille (per 1000 impressions).

entry.title: ROAS — ROW ass
entry.summary: ROAS is pronounced "ROW ass" (/ˈroʊ æs/). "ROW-as" most common in performance marketing; some spell out. Return On Ad Spend.

entry.title: MoM — M O M
entry.summary: MoM is pronounced "M O M" (/ˌɛm oʊ ˈɛm/). "M-O-M" letters common in dashboards; "mom" (rhymes with bomb) sometimes spoken. Month-over-Month.

entry.title: YoY — Y O Y
entry.summary: YoY is pronounced "Y O Y" (/ˌwaɪ oʊ ˈwaɪ/). Letters "Y-O-Y". Year-over-Year.

entry.title: QoQ — Q O Q
entry.summary: QoQ is pronounced "Q O Q" (/ˌkjuː oʊ ˈkjuː/). Letters "Q-O-Q". Quarter-over-Quarter.

entry.title: NDA — N D A
entry.summary: NDA is pronounced "N D A" (/ˌɛn diː ˈeɪ/). Letters "N-D-A". Non-Disclosure Agreement.

entry.title: MSA — M S A
entry.summary: MSA is pronounced "M S A" (/ˌɛm ɛs ˈeɪ/). Letters "M-S-A". Master Service Agreement.

entry.title: SOW — sow
entry.summary: SOW is pronounced "sow" (/saʊ/). "sow" (rhymes with cow) — standard in consulting/sales. Some spell out. Statement of Work.

entry.title: RFP — R F P
entry.summary: RFP is pronounced "R F P" (/ˌɑːr ɛf ˈpiː/). Letters "R-F-P". Request For Proposal.

entry.title: NLP — N L P
entry.summary: NLP is pronounced "N L P" (/ˌɛn ɛl ˈpiː/). Letters "N-L-P". Natural Language Processing (also Neuro-Linguistic Programming, same pronunciation).

entry.title: PII — P I I
entry.summary: PII is pronounced "P I I" (/ˌpiː aɪ ˈaɪ/). "P-I-I" letter-by-letter standard in compliance/legal; "pie" sometimes heard casually. Personally Identifiable Information.
